response:
Emily gathered 24 blueberries which became the starting point for their muffin making adventure. David collected 15 more than Emily which meant he had 39 blueberries in total. His amount was also three times what Sophia had so Sophia must have collected 13 blueberries. Together they had a total of 76 blueberries that they used for their muffins.

***

They divided their blueberries into batches with each batch needing 9 blueberries. The total number of batches they could make was 8 since 76 divided by 9 gives 8 full batches. Each batch was sold for 5 dollars which meant they had a chance to earn a nice amount from their efforts. The process of making and selling muffins brought them closer as they worked together.

***

The money they made was calculated by multiplying the number of batches by the price per batch. This resulted in 40 dollars from the muffins they sold. The amount they earned was a reflection of their combined effort and the number of blueberries they collected. Every step from gathering to selling was part of their journey.

***

Their experience with blueberries and muffins taught them the value of teamwork and hard work. The money they earned was a reward for their time and effort. They learned that small actions can lead to meaningful outcomes. The joy of their youth was evident in every step of the process. Youth.
